title: SemanticMatchingPlanche
description: One planche for the Semantic Matching task. The patient sees a central target image and two peripheral choice images; they must touch the one that belongs to the same semantic category as the target.

stimulus_id.description: Unique planche identifier. Format: SM_NNN (e.g. SM_001).

correct_response.description: Which side is the semantically correct choice.

left_right_balance.description: Mirror of correct_response. Used explicitly for counterbalancing checks.

is_excluded.description: Set true to permanently remove this planche from all sets.

center_image.description: Path to the central (target) image, relative to stimuli/images/.

left_image.description: Path to the left choice image, relative to stimuli/images/.

right_image.description: Path to the right choice image, relative to stimuli/images/.

left_label.description: Human-readable label for the left image.

right_label.description: Human-readable label for the right image.

semantic_category.description: Semantic category shared by center and correct choice.

distractor_type.description: How the distractor relates to the target. 'semantic' = same superordinate category; 'perceptual' = visually similar; 'unrelated' = no obvious link.

difficulty.description: Clinician-rated difficulty: 1 = trivially easy, 5 = very hard.
